items.title: Built in Fort Worth: Wistron Opens Advanced Manufacturing Plant to Produce NVIDIA AI Systems
items.content_text: The AI era runs on AI infrastructure. Many of these advanced systems are built and tested in Texas. Wistron opened its first U.S. manufacturing facility today in Fort Worth — a 324,000-square-foot greenfield plant producing superchips at the heart of some of the world’s most capable AI systems. In front of an audience of Wistron […]

items.title: Introducing Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber
items.content_text: Google introduces Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber to help defenders find, validate, and patch software vulnerabilities quickly and efficiently.

items.title: NVIDIA and Japan Bring Full-Stack AI and Robotics to Every Industry
items.content_text: NVIDIA and its partners in Japan are this week showcasing the AI ecosystem's latest advancements. Check back here for updates.

items.title: Nemotron Labs: How Open Models Give Enterprises and Nations AI They Can Trust, Control and Customize
items.content_text: Learn how NVIDIA Nemotron open models help enterprises build specialized AI they can trust, control and customize with accuracy, efficiency and flexibility.

items.title: AI in Indian Education: Atal Innovation Mission and Google launch ATL Saathi
items.content_text: Atal Innovation Mission launches ATL Saathi, a Gemini powered AI assistant empowering India's educators to nurture the next generation of innovators.

items.title: Celebrating 25 years of visual search innovation
items.content_text: Google Images is turning 25. Here’s a look back at some major milestones — and new ways to explore and create visual content.

items.title: Scaling cryptographic verification to boost computer security
items.content_text: Cryptographic code supports vital protections in modern computing systems. Learn how a new method helps verify code as developers write it while preserving speed and adaptability as it gets implemented and evolves:

items.title: Amazon and University of Michigan give robots a sense of touch
items.content_text: HydroShear, a new physics-based simulator, teaches robots how to use their sense of touch to perform complex manipulation tasks, in a way that transfers seamlessly to the real world.

items.title: Aurora 1.5: Extending open foundation models for weather and Earth-system applications - Microsoft Research
items.content_text: Aurora 1.5 adds 22 more variables, hourly temporal resolution, and probabilistic ensemble forecasting to the Aurora foundation model, making it more useful for real-world weather, climate, and energy applications.

items.title: Capturing token IDs during agentic interactions for better reinforcement learning
items.content_text: A new Rust proxy called Turnstile sits between the model backend and the agent harness to capture information lost in mere text transcripts.

items.content_text: Agentic systems often face a trade-off between accuracy and cost. The highest-performing proprietary frontier models and harnesses provide top accuracy but are expensive. Fine-tuning offers one way to…

items.title: AI can generate Charts. Flint helps generate better ones.
items.content_text: Short chart specifications are easy to write, but often produce uninspiring results. Flint is an open-source visualization language that offers a middle path, letting AI agents create expressive charts from compact, human-editable specifications.

items.content_text: Agentic systems turn model reasoning into action through multi-step workflows that combine inference, tool use, code execution, retrieval, orchestration, and result handling. As these systems scale…

items.title: Expanding Managed Agents in Gemini API:  background tasks, remote MCP and more
items.content_text: We’re announcing new capabilities in Managed Agents in Gemini API so developers can build reliable, production-ready agents.

items.title: The latest AI news we announced in June 2026
items.content_text: Here are Google’s latest AI updates from June 2026.

items.title: New York City educators and industry leaders gathered at Google’s offices to shape the future of AI in classrooms.
items.content_text: Google, the New York Jobs CEO Council and Urban Assembly hosted an AI summit for 150 education and industry leaders.

items.title: SkillOpt turns AI agent skills into trainable assets
items.content_text: AI agents often fail because their instructions, or skills, are manually modified with no guarantee of improvement. Learn how SkillOpt turns skill editing into a training process, making agent behavior more reliable without changing model weights:

items.title: Unlocking Britain’s next era of productivity: Building a nation of AI trailblazers
items.content_text: Google UK shares its latest Economic Impact Report and how to enable more people to unlock the benefits of AI-powered technologies.

items.title: Memora scales agent memory to boost long-horizon productivity
items.content_text: AI agents can't remember past conversations. They must constantly reload or retrieve context, which grows less efficient as tasks get longer and more complex. Memora solves this with a scalable memory system separating what’s stored from how it's retrieved.

items.title: Ask an AI expert: What exactly is the full stack?
items.content_text: A Google expert explains what it means to take a full-stack approach to AI and why it’s been the foundation of our AI work for so long.

items.title: Turning brain prediction models into testable explanations
items.content_text: Researchers introduce generative causal testing, which translates black box models into clear hypotheses and verifies them in the scanner, revealing what specific brain regions respond to in language.

items.title: Our latest Google Finance upgrades, including a new app
items.content_text: The new Google Finance is coming out of beta and launching a new Android app.

items.title: The fuel of the future is already here: Why TRISO matters
items.content_text: Millimeter-scale particles of nuclear-reactor fuel are encased in four layers of different materials that act as a “miniature containment system”.

items.title: Talos brings continuous genomic reanalysis to nearly 5,000 unsolved cases
items.content_text: Talos was built to help resolve a major bottleneck in genomic medicine: human review time. The open-source system recovered 90% of in-scope diagnoses while surfacing just 1.3 candidate variants per patient for expert review.

items.title: Securing internal systems against increasingly capable and imperfectly aligned AI
items.content_text: Discover our AI Control Roadmap: a defense-in-depth system to securely manage advanced, potentially misaligned AI agents.

items.title: New research shows how AMIE, our medical AI, could help manage health conditions.
items.content_text: Research in “Nature” shows our conversational AI system matches primary care physicians in complex disease management.

items.title: Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning
items.content_text: Google DeepMind is working alongside the UK government to co-develop an AI-powered prototype to help cut application decision times by 50%.

items.title: Inside Project Ire’s discovery of an evasive malware sample
items.content_text: Project Ire examined a timely malware sample and determined its intent through reverse engineering—identifying LOTUSLITE characteristics even as most major EDR tools did not detect it.

items.title: Google DeepMind and partners announce multi-agent safety research funding call.
items.content_text: Google DeepMind and partners are announcing a new technical research funding call of up to $10M for researchers worldwide to strengthen multi-agent safety.

items.title: Graviton5’s improved design increases speed and energy efficiency — beyond Moore’s law
items.content_text: A new chiplet architecture, custom die-to-die connectivity, and support for DDR5-8800 memory and the latest PCIe gen6 interconnects improve performance by 25% for general-purpose and agentic AI workloads.

items.title: EC2’s formally verified “isolation engine” provides mathematical assurance of virtual-machine isolation
items.content_text: Splitting the “separation kernel” off from the rest of the Nitro security system and using only a subset of the Rust programming language to code it enabled its formal verification.

items.title: Real-world grounding in agentic AI
items.content_text: Four approaches can dramatically improve the performance and trustworthiness of AI agents in operational environments.

items.title: Bridging intent and execution in agentic systems
items.content_text: The harnesses that mediate between models and tools in agentic systems are becoming their own performance bottleneck, but a few simple design principles can fix what ails them.

items.title: Data Formulator 0.7: AI-powered data analytics for enterprise data
items.content_text: Data Formulator introduces AI-powered analytics for enterprise data workflows. Data teams can easily bring enterprise data into an AI-ready workspace where users can explore, analyze, and visualize data with AI agents to turn raw data into actionable insights:

items.title: Extending human intelligence through AI
items.content_text: Understanding AI as an extension of human intelligence—not a replacement for it—offers a more grounded path for building trustworthy AI systems.

items.title: AWS and Gray Lab at Johns Hopkins Whiting School of Engineering announce groundbreaking database for AI/ML antibody design
items.content_text: The Antibody Developability Benchmark is powered by one of the most diverse antibody datasets, enabling transparent performance evaluation for AI-guided antibody design.

items.title: How AI is changing the nature of mathematical research
items.content_text: What machine learning theorists learned using AI agents to generate proofs — and what comes next.

items.title: SIMA 2: A Gemini-Powered AI Agent for 3D Virtual Worlds
items.content_text: Introducing SIMA 2, the next milestone in our research creating general and helpful AI agents. By integrating the advanced capabilities of our Gemini models, SIMA is evolving from an instruction-follower into an interactive gaming companion.

items.title: Autonomous Swarming Takes Flight: The Next Era of UAV Operations
items.content_text: How Palladyne AI's edge-based swarming redefines UAV operations. True drone teamwork that boosts agility, resilience, and mission efficiency.

items.title: Amazon and Carnegie Mellon University launch strategic AI Innovation Hub
items.content_text: The collaboration will advance research in generative AI, robotics, natural-language processing and cloud computing while fostering innovation in foundational and emerging technologies.
